text: Ne̱ ɔɔ be̱ŋŋaa bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ɔnyare̱ ya kine mò̱ ka, ne̱ ɔɔ ya waare̱e̱ ɔkye̱e̱ baŋbaŋ, ɔɔ waa bɔye̱. Mò̱ ke̱be̱e̱ waare̱e̱ ɔkye̱e̱ baŋbaŋ‑ɔ a sa ne̱ ɔɔ waa kakye̱e̱kpa.
translated: This is what the wise man said: "Listen to me, this is the way to do things properly. If you follow this way, then this is the right path."

ref: he took the seven loaves of bread and the fish and gave thanks. He then broke them and handed them to his disciples, who passed them around to the crowds.
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ taa bodobodoo akuri asunoo amo̱ na ŋkiŋgyi amo̱, ne̱ ɔɔ sa Wuribware̱ aŋsɛ we̱e̱ se̱. Ne̱ ɔɔ te̱ŋŋe̱‑rɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ taa sa mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ fe̱yɛ bo̱ ke‑ro sa bamo̱. Ne̱ baa ke‑ro sa ase̱sɛ‑ɔ pɛɛɛ.
translated: He went to Bodobodoo with the ŋkiŋgyi people and said to Wuribware, "Go to them." He returned and went to the house of Agyasepo-ɔ and spoke to the people. Then they went to the elders and said, "Look!"

ref: You're nothing but show-offs! First, take the log out of your own eye; then you can see how to take the speck out of your friend's eye.
text: Fo̱ nnɔ ŋnyɔ ŋnyɔ wuye mɔ, lee ke̱be̱ŋtiŋ dabe̱ ne̱ ke̱ dɔŋ fo̱ fɔŋfɔŋ ke̱katɔ se̱‑ɔ pwɛɛ na fo̱ dɛɛ taare̱ ŋu dame̱naŋsɛ lee kakpafɛɛ ne̱ ka dɔŋ fo̱ bɛɛko̱ lee si‑o.
translated: The people arrived, bringing gifts to present to the chief to honor him with a song. The chief welcomed the people, and they danced, gave food to the elders, and ate.

ref: Jesus and his disciples went to his home in Capernaum. After they were inside the house, Jesus asked them, “What were you arguing about along the way?”
text: Ne̱ baa ba Kapaaniyum maŋ‑nɔ. Bamo̱ a lwee Yeesuu lɔŋ‑nɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ oo bise mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ fe̱yɛ, “Ntɔ ke̱tɔ se̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ i gyiiri ikii kpa‑rɔ‑ɔ ne̱e̱?”
translated: And they said the Leader has gone. They said Jesus has come, and he asked them, "Why did you come to this place where there is no one?"

text: Amaa ke̱yaafɔre̱ ko̱ mɔ a kpuriti waagya laare̱‑rɔ ɔ gya bamo̱ se̱. Ne̱ ba kpa a bo̱ kra mò̱ gbaa mò̱.
translated: "Amaa, the hunter, went to the village and saw a snake. But he told me that he was afraid and the snake bit him."

ref: Some other seeds fell where thornbushes grew up and choked the plants.
text: Ayaabraagyi‑o ako̱ ne̱ aa le̱e̱ da e̱fa‑rɔ‑ɔ mɔ a ko̱so̱ daŋ. Amaa e̱fa a daŋ mwiire amo̱ se̱.
translated: Ayaabraagyi does not eat it, the one who eats it. He who eats it, the one who is eaten.

translated: Hear, O people, the Lord has spoken, the book of the Lord's words has been given to you, and the Lord's words are with you. The book of the Lord has been revealed to you. translated: He said, "Come and see where the Lord has been staying." So they came and saw where He was staying, and they stayed with Him that day. Then Andrew, Simon's brother, found his own brother Peter and brought him to Jesus. (John 1:39-41)

text: Ne̱ ɔɔ be̱e̱ yɔ ya ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱. Ne̱ ɔ yɛ, “N se̱, mo̱ awo̱re̱fɔɔ ne̱ mo̱ i gyi‑o e̱ ma gye̱ kɛɛ, na mo̱ŋ gyi amo̱ lo̱we̱ wu fe̱raa, ane̱ŋ ne̱ fo̱ e̱ kpa‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ a waa.”
translated: And the Lord said to him, "I am the Lord, who brought you out of Egypt and led you through the wilderness, and I have cursed you, but your descendants will be many, and your name will be remembered forever."
